vol.seriousTimer: SERIOUS incident timer: {s}s remaining until safety override.

ops.title: MASTER TERMINAL · StadiaChat
ops.planningHint: Plan posts by place — assign volunteers to gates, sections, and facilities before and during matchday.
ops.planDescPh: e.g. Guide ADA guests, keep queue moving, radio channel 1
ops.noPlans: No plans yet. Create a post with a place of assistance above.
ops.criticalFeedHint: Only serious / emergency traffic (Cat D). Full ops history is on Full timeline.
ops.noCritical: No Category D messages right now.
ops.timelineIncidentHint: Cat C and Cat D open incidents for this stadium. Critical desk also on Cat D page.
profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Language preference applies to the whole interface and AI answers.
fan.title: Fan Voice Assist
fan.subtitle: Point the mic at the fan. AI detects language, answers from stadium protocols, and speaks the reply aloud.
fan.coachingTitle: Volunteer assist required — your actions (not spoken)
fan.typeFallback: Or type fan question if mic unavailable…
fan.unsupported: This browser does not support speech recognition. Use Chrome/Edge on HTTPS or localhost, or type the fan question below.
fan.noSpeech: No speech captured. Hold the mic and try again.
fan.micDenied: Microphone permission denied. Allow mic access in the browser.
fan.notSecureTitle: Mic blocked on this URL (Not secure)
fan.notSecure: Browsers only allow the microphone on https:// or http://localhost — not LAN IPs like 192.168.x.x. Open http://localhost:3000 for mic, or type the fan question below (text always works).
home.tagline: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Stadium Ops AI
home.badge: Secure operational channel · Not a public fan chatbot
home.heroBody: StadiaChat is the AI Core ops interface for FIFA World Cup 2026 volunteers and venue Operations Leads. It answers protocol FAQs instantly, escalates floor incidents to the right Lead, deploys emergency SOPs with zero latency, and helps volunteers assist multilingual fans via mic and speaker — all isolated per stadium.
home.sessionBound: Session bound to stadium_id · PIN · role
home.regNoPin: No stadium PIN needed to register. After your Ops Lead approves, log in with the stadium PIN.
home.demoRegHint: Pending until Ops Lead approves — then login with PIN.
home.demoOpsHint: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 · language saved on Lead
home.aboutTitle: Built for matchday chaos — not generic chatbot demos
home.purposeBody: Give every stadium volunteer a fast, context-aware operational interface: official protocols in their language, clear escalation to the Operations Lead, and critical safety paths that never wait on a busy radio channel. One Lead per stadium runs a master terminal for approvals, tasks, and incident control.
home.not1: Not a public tourism or city guide chatbot
home.not2: Not a replacement for emergency medical/security command
home.not3: Not a free-form social chat between fans
home.not4: Not allowed to use maps or rosters from another stadium_id
home.catADesc: Official answer from local stadium library, localized.
home.catBDesc: Hard reject → Fan Guide app. No free-form city advice.
home.catCDesc: Alert card to Ops Lead + 3 remediation checkboxes.
home.catDDesc: Emergency SOP or Lead alert + 300s safety override.
home.feat1Body: Every session is locked to a stadium_id, PIN, and role. No cross-venue data bleed.
home.feat2Body: Volunteer messages auto-route: protocol FAQs, out-of-scope reject, minor incidents to Ops Lead, or critical emergency protocols.
home.feat3Body: Approve volunteers, assign task cards, authorize 3-option remediations, and handle serious alerts with a 300s safety override.
home.feat4Body: Mic + speaker for multilingual fans. Answers in their language; coaches the volunteer in chat when hands-on help is required.
home.faqIntro: Product answers for judges, construction & deploy answers for engineers. Full written guide: docs/FAQ.md.
home.ja1: World Cup venues run thousands of multilingual volunteers. Radio and ad-hoc chat are noisy, unsafe for emergencies, and can leak data across sites. StadiaChat is a tenancy-safe AI ops channel: official SOP answers instantly, escalates only what needs a human Lead, deploys emergency protocols under pressure, and helps volunteers assist fans in their language via Fan Voice.
home.ja2: FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteers and one Operations Lead per stadium — not a public fan chatbot. Fans are helped through the volunteer’s device (mic + speaker). Six demo venues are seeded (MetLife, SoFi, AT&T, BC Place, Azteca, Hard Rock).
home.jq3: What should I demo in 2 minutes?
home.ja3: 1) Volunteer login: Alex Rivera · metlife_2026 · PIN WC26-MET. 2) Ask: “Where is the ADA elevator to Gate 3?” (Cat A protocol FAQ). 3) Report: “Low brochure inventory at Gate 1” (Cat C → Ops Lead). 4) Ops Lead: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 — approve remediations. 5) Fan Voice: Spanish facility question → spoken answer + volunteer coaching when needed.
home.jq4: How does safety work for critical incidents?
home.ja4: Category D: if a matching emergency protocol exists (medical, missing child, fire, unattended bag, etc.), it deploys to the volunteer immediately and bypasses the Lead. If not, the Lead gets a critical alert + 3 options and a 300-second timer. No Lead action → automatic safety directive to the volunteer.
home.jq5: What stadium protocol FAQs can volunteers ask?
home.ja5: Sixteen Cat A topics per stadium (same topics, venue landmarks differ): ADA/elevators, restrooms, tickets, food/concessions, merch, guest services, water/ATM/Wi‑Fi/prayer, medical posts, prohibited items, re-entry, smoking, queues, accessibility+, radio channels, shift/credential, zone access. Eleven Cat D emergencies (medical, missing child, crush, water hazard, fire, bag, fight, pitch invasion, active threat, weather, structural). Full list: docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md.
home.jq6: Where does it run and is data durable?
home.ja6: Source on GitHub. Local: npm run dev with optional file DB. Production: deployed as a Next.js Web Service on Render with MongoDB Atlas (MONGODB_URI) so users, messages, and incidents survive restarts. Check GET /api/health/storage → active_backend mongodb. Demo credentials work on the live URL the same as local.
home.dq1: How do I run it locally (construction)?
home.da1: See docs/SETUP.md. Node 20+: git clone → npm install → copy .env.example to .env.local → set GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY → npm run dev → http://localhost:3000. Optional MONGODB_URI for durable local data. Without an AI key, heuristics + protocol library still work.
home.da2: docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md. Quick: volunteer Alex Rivera + metlife_2026 + PIN WC26-MET. Ops Lead: metlife_2026 + ops_metlife_2026. Register needs no PIN; login after Ops approval requires the stadium PIN.
home.da3: src/lib/orchestrator.ts (A–D routing), src/lib/fan-assist.ts (voice + coaching), src/lib/xai.ts (Google AI Studio multi-model cascade), src/lib/protocol-pack.ts (seeded FAQs/emergencies), src/lib/memory.ts (Mongo memory). Canonical rules: prompts/system-prompt.md.
home.da5: Local default: data/db.json (gitignored). Production/Render: set MONGODB_URI (Atlas) — app_state + memory_events + mirrored collections. Tenancy always filters by stadium_id. POST /api/admin/reset reseeds. After protocol code changes, reset so new pack loads.
home.da6: A: Where is the nearest ADA elevator to Gate 3? / Where can I buy food near section 110? · B: Best restaurants near the stadium downtown? (out of scope → Fan Guide) · C: Low brochure inventory at Gate 1 · D: Missing child near Gate 3 / medical distress section 112 / unattended bag by restrooms.
home.dq7: How do I deploy to Render?
home.da7: docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md. Web Service (not Static): Build npm install && npm run build · Start npm start · Branch main. Env: MONGODB_URI (required), GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY (recommended), MONGODB_DB=stadiachat. Atlas Network Access 0.0.0.0/0. Verify GET /api/health/storage shows mongodb.
home.da8: docs/SETUP.md (local build), docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md (Render), docs/FAQ.md (this FAQ in long form), docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md, docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md, README.md. Secrets only in .env.local or Render Environment — never commit.
home.readyBody: Use demo credentials above — locally (npm run dev) or on your Render URL with MongoDB connected.
home.footer: StadiaChat · FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteer & Ops Lead prototype · Tenancy-enforced AI Core · GitHub + Render
